title: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 3
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 27-40, which are based on Reading Passage 3 below.

The Case for Imperfect Design

A café owner notices that one of the tables near the window has begun to wobble. A customer offers to fix it with a folded piece of card, and soon three people are discussing whether the floor is uneven, whether the table leg is too short, and whether the table should be replaced. Nothing dramatic has happened. Yet the small fault has made the object visible. For a few minutes, the table is no longer just background furniture. It is a thing with parts, balance, age and history.

Modern design often tries to prevent this kind of attention. Many products are made to appear smooth, sealed and effortless. Buttons disappear into touchscreens; screws are hidden; instructions are reduced to diagrams; repairs are discouraged or made difficult. The attraction is obvious. A well-designed object should not require its user to become an engineer. Few people want a printer, ticket machine or school laptop that demands constant interpretation. Smooth design can reduce errors, save time and make technology available to people who lack specialist knowledge.

However, there is a difference between helpful simplicity and complete invisibility. When an object hides all signs of how it works, users may become efficient but helpless. They can operate the object while it behaves as expected, but they have little idea what to do when it fails. Design researcher Selin Arat calls this problem "sealed convenience". In her view, some products remove not only unnecessary difficulty but also useful understanding. They give people access, but not agency.

Arat and her colleagues explored this issue in a study involving 110 volunteers. Participants were asked to assemble a small desk lamp. One group received a version with concealed fastenings and a single sheet of highly simplified instructions. A second group received a version with visible screws, labelled parts and a minor wiring error that could be corrected by following a diagram. The first group assembled the lamp more quickly and reported less frustration. But when both groups were later given a different lamp with a loose internal connection, the second group was more successful at identifying the problem. The researchers did not conclude that products should be made difficult. Rather, they argued that limited, recoverable friction can help users develop a practical understanding of an object.

The same principle can apply beyond domestic products. Some software companies have begun to reconsider interfaces that hide every process from the user. A fully automatic system may be convenient, but it can also make mistakes difficult to notice. In one accounting program studied by Arat's team, users who were shown a brief explanation of how the software classified expenses were slower at first, but better at detecting later errors. Users who saw only the final category accepted more incorrect classifications. The explanation acted like a visible screw: it interrupted smoothness, but it also created understanding.

Environmental designers make a related argument about repair. Objects that cannot be opened are more likely to be discarded when a single part fails. By contrast, products with replaceable batteries, labelled components or accessible fastenings invite users to imagine repair as possible. This does not mean that everyone will repair everything. Time, cost and safety matter. But the visible possibility of repair can change a person's relationship with an object. A device becomes less like a sealed service and more like a material thing that can be maintained.

Critics of imperfect design are right to be cautious. Difficulty can exclude people, especially those with limited time, money or physical ability. A badly designed medical device, transport system or public website cannot be defended on the grounds that it teaches patience. The aim is not to make life harder. The question is where to place the boundary between unnecessary obstruction and useful participation.

The most thoughtful designers, then, are not those who worship either smoothness or difficulty. They ask what kind of user the object is creating. A product that works flawlessly until it becomes useless may be less generous than one that occasionally asks its user to notice, adjust and understand. Imperfection, carefully designed, can be a form of invitation.

Questions 27-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-30 on your answer sheet.

27 The writer describes the wobbling café table in order to show that
A small faults can make people notice how objects work.
B public furniture is usually poorly maintained.
C customers often know more about repair than owners.
D old objects are more attractive than new ones.

28 What does the writer say about smooth modern design in the second paragraph?
A It is mainly intended to make products look expensive.
B It can make objects easier for non-specialists to use.
C It usually increases the number of user errors.
D It is disliked by people who use technology regularly.

29 What does Selin Arat mean by "sealed convenience"?
A A kind of design that is useful only in domestic settings.
B A design approach that gives users access but little understanding.
C A method of protecting products from physical damage.
D A system that allows users to repair objects without help.

30 What did Arat's lamp study find?
A The group with simplified instructions understood the second lamp better.
B The group with visible parts performed better on a later repair task.
C Both groups showed equal ability to identify the loose connection.
D Participants preferred the lamp that contained no visible screws.

Questions 31-35
Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A-I, below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, in boxes 31-35 on your answer sheet.

Imperfect design and user understanding

In Arat's lamp study, the first group used concealed fastenings and simple instructions, so they completed the first task with less 31 ____________. However, the second group had to deal with visible parts and a 32 ____________ that could be fixed. This helped them develop a more 33 ____________ understanding of the object. A similar argument is made about software: showing users how decisions are made may slow them down initially, but can make them better at detecting 34 ____________. In both cases, a small interruption to smoothness can create 35 ____________.

A frustration
B minor wiring error
C practical
D later errors
E understanding
F hidden screen
G specialist training
H attractive appearance
I permanent failure

Questions 36-40
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

36 The writer believes that all products should be made more difficult to use.
37 The writer suggests that completely hidden processes can make users less able to respond when something goes wrong.
38 People who repair objects always spend less money than people who replace them.
39 The writer accepts that badly designed public systems cannot be justified simply because they create difficulty.
40 The writer believes that the best designers should always remove every sign of imperfection.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 27: The writer describes the wobbling café table in order to show that

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: Yet the small fault has made the object visible. For a few minutes, the table is no longer just background furniture. It is a thing with parts, balance, age and history.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะ wobbling table ทำให้ the small fault has made the object visible และคนเห็น parts, balance, age and history ตรง small faults can make people notice how objects work ตัวเลือก B ผิดเพราะไม่ได้สรุปว่าเฟอร์นิเจอร์สาธารณะดูแลไม่ดี ตัวเลือก C ผิดเพราะไม่ได้เปรียบความรู้ลูกค้ากับเจ้าของ ตัวเลือก D ผิดเพราะไม่ได้พูดว่าวัตถุเก่าสวยกว่าใหม่

Paraphrased Vocabulary: notice how objects work = made the object visible / thing with parts; small faults = small fault; answer signal = A



Question 28: What does the writer say about smooth modern design in the second paragraph?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: Smooth design can reduce errors, save time and make technology available to people who lack specialist knowledge.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะย่อหน้าสองบอก smooth design ช่วย reduce errors และ make technology available to people who lack specialist knowledge ตรง easier for non-specialists ตัวเลือก A ผิดเพราะไม่ได้พูดเรื่องดูแพง ตัวเลือก C ผิดเพราะ passage บอก reduce errors ไม่ใช่ increase errors ตัวเลือก D ผิดเพราะไม่ได้กล่าวว่าคนใช้เทคโนโลยีไม่ชอบ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: easier for non-specialists = available to people who lack specialist knowledge; smooth design = smooth, sealed and effortless; answer signal = B



Question 29: What does Selin Arat mean by "sealed convenience"?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: When an object hides all signs of how it works, users may become efficient but helpless... They give people access, but not agency.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะ sealed convenience หมายถึง users ได้ access แต่ไม่มี agency/understanding เมื่อซ่อนวิธีทำงาน ตัวเลือก A ผิดเพราะหลักการใช้กับ software และ domestic ตัวเลือก C ผิดเพราะไม่ได้พูดป้องกันความเสียหายทางกาย ตัวเลือก D ผิดเพราะ design นี้ทำให้ helpless เมื่อเสีย ไม่ใช่ช่วยซ่อมเอง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: access but little understanding = access, but not agency; sealed convenience = hides all signs of how it works; answer signal = B



Question 30: What did Arat's lamp study find?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: when both groups were later given a different lamp with a loose internal connection, the second group was more successful at identifying the problem.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะ second group (visible parts) ระบุ loose connection ได้ดีกว่าใน later task ตัวเลือก A ผิดเพราะ first group เร็วกว่าในขั้นแรก ไม่ได้เข้าใจ lamp ที่สองดีกว่า ตัวเลือก C ผิดเพราะ second group สำเร็จกว่า ไม่เท่ากัน ตัวเลือก D ผิดเพราะไม่ได้พูด preference

Paraphrased Vocabulary: visible parts = visible screws, labelled parts; later repair task = different lamp with loose internal connection; answer signal = B



Question 31: they completed the first task with less .................... .

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: The first group assembled the lamp more quickly and reported less frustration.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A (frustration) เพราะ first group reported less frustration ตรง less ___ ใน summary ไม่ใช่ H attractive appearance หรือ G specialist training

Paraphrased Vocabulary: less ___ = less frustration; word bank = A frustration



Question 32: the second group had to deal with visible parts and a .................... that could be fixed.

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: a minor wiring error that could be corrected by following a diagram.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B (minor wiring error) เพราะ second group มี minor wiring error that could be corrected ตรง ___ that could be fixed ไม่ใช่ I permanent failure

Paraphrased Vocabulary: ___ that could be fixed = minor wiring error; word bank = B minor wiring error



Question 33: This helped them develop a more .................... understanding of the object.

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: limited, recoverable friction can help users develop a practical understanding of an object.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C (practical) เพราะ researchers ว่า friction ช่วย develop a practical understanding ตรง more ___ understanding ไม่ใช่ E understanding ที่เป็นผลในข้อ 35

Paraphrased Vocabulary: more ___ understanding = practical understanding; word bank = C practical



Question 34: can make them better at detecting .................... .

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: users who were shown a brief explanation of how the software classified expenses were slower at first, but better at detecting later errors.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ D (later errors) เพราะ users better at detecting later errors ใน accounting software ไม่ใช่ B minor wiring error ที่เป็นตัวอย่าง lamp

Paraphrased Vocabulary: detecting ___ = detecting later errors; word bank = D later errors



Question 35: a small interruption to smoothness can create .................... .

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: The explanation acted like a visible screw: it interrupted smoothness, but it also created understanding.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E (understanding) เพราะ interruption to smoothness created understanding ตรง create ___ ไม่ใช่ C practical ที่เป็นคำคุณในข้อ 33

Paraphrased Vocabulary: create ___ = created understanding; word bank = E understanding



Question 36: The writer believes that all products should be made more difficult to use.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: The researchers did not conclude that products should be made difficult.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะ researchers did not conclude that products should be made difficult ขัดกับ all products should be more difficult ถ้า YES จะข้ามประโยคปฏิเสธนี้

Paraphrased Vocabulary: all products more difficult = products should be made difficult; writer claim = NO



Question 37: The writer suggests that completely hidden processes can make users less able to respond when something goes wrong.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: When an object hides all signs of how it works, users may become efficient but helpless. They can operate the object while it behaves as expected, but they have little idea what to do when it fails.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะ writer บอกเมื่อ hides all signs of how it works users become helpless และ have little idea what to do when it fails ตรง less able to respond when something goes wrong

Paraphrased Vocabulary: hidden processes = hides all signs of how it works; less able to respond = helpless / little idea what to do when it fails



Question 38: People who repair objects always spend less money than people who replace them.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: Time, cost and safety matter.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage พูด Time, cost and safety matter และ repair is possible แต่ไม่เปรียบเทียบว่า repair always spends less than replace คำว่า always less money ไม่มีหลักฐาน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: always spend less = not compared; NOT GIVEN = cost matters but no repair vs replace comparison



Question 39: The writer accepts that badly designed public systems cannot be justified simply because they create difficulty.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: A badly designed medical device, transport system or public website cannot be defended on the grounds that it teaches patience.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะ badly designed medical device, transport system or public website cannot be defended on the grounds that it teaches patience ตรงข้อความโจทย์

Paraphrased Vocabulary: badly designed public systems = medical device, transport system, public website; create difficulty = teaches patience



Question 40: The writer believes that the best designers should always remove every sign of imperfection.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 3 - The Case for Imperfect Design

Exact Portion: The most thoughtful designers, then, are not those who worship either smoothness or difficulty. Imperfection, carefully designed, can be a form of invitation.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะ thoughtful designers ไม่ worship smoothness only และ imperfection carefully designed can be a form of invitation ขัดกับ remove every sign of imperfection

Paraphrased Vocabulary: remove every imperfection = worship either smoothness only; best designers = most thoughtful designers
